title: pinguicula corsica subvar. coerulescens
intro: Pinguicula corsica subvar. coerulescens, also known as the Corsican butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the Pinguicula genus. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pinguicula corsica subvar. coerulescens include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant have been used in the past, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy, and it is often used as an ingredient in traditional remedies and salves.
